title: JSDF rifles in EoE
wikitext:
[]: JSDF rifles in EoE             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 4:46 pm When the JSDF invades NERV in EoE, did anyone notice the type of rifles they were using? Oddly, it's NOT the Howa Type 89, which has been the standard-issue service rifle of the JSDF since 1989. It's the Heckler & Koch G11. Like the Steyr on which the pallet rifles are based, was one of the four finalists in the ACR competition, none of which got past the prototype stages. (G11 is second from top, pallet rifle should be obvious) Was Anno obsessed with the ACR program or something?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:07 pm Nothing new here, and considering that it's an entirely fictional branch of the SDF which does the invading, the real-world service of the Types 64 and 89 doesn't count for much. Gainax's mechanical designer(s) probably used the pre-production G11 for the same reason that they used the Desert Eagle and AI Arctic Warfare: they looked cool. Certainly no real force would be fielding that version of the G11 - the greatly improved G11K2 has been around since at least the early 90s.

[]:             Quote Post by jim_loves_randy » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:24 pm In real life, do special forces often use flamethrowers to supplement their guns in an invasion?

[]:             Quote Post by Formless One » Sun Nov 16, 2008 5:29 pm No. Flamethrowers are mostly used to smoke out fortifications or caves. Their short range and bulkiness makes them impractical for widespread deployment when grenades and fully automatic fire can be used for clearing rooms, while there are other bunker busting methods as well.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Nov 16, 2008 8:16 pm Yeah, it seems like flamethrowers are kind of outdated anyway. They're just badass, thats all.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 16, 2008 8:27 pm And they're good to have around just in case you encounter giant, acid-bleeding bugs.

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Nov 16, 2008 8:29 pm Of course. Nothing is better than fire (which is man's best friend, not dogs)

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Nov 16, 2008 8:39 pm jim_loves_randy wrote: In real life, do special forces often use flamethrowers to supplement their guns in an invasion?

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Sun Nov 16, 2008 8:53 pm Reichu wrote: And they're good to have around just in case you encounter giant, acid-bleeding bugs.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:16 pm jim_loves_randy wrote: In real life, do special forces often use flamethrowers to supplement their guns in an invasion?

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:28 pm ^Count A'ight Those Mk77s work on thermobaric principles, of course newer payloads will incorporate more efficient compounds like phosphorus.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:36 pm Synapsid wrote: ^Count A'ight Those Mk77s work on thermobaric principles

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Nov 16, 2008 9:38 pm yes I've known its a G11 for a while

[]:             Quote Post by LeoXiao » Sun Nov 16, 2008 10:04 pm [quote="Synapsid"][quote="jim_loves_randy"] they were used in the second Falluja campaign to destroy fortifications. Of course such a action was controversial as such weapons kill and destroy anything nearby Holy f*ck I forgot about those. They are more badass than flamethrowers

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Nov 16, 2008 10:28 pm Reichu wrote: And they're good to have around just in case you encounter giant, acid-bleeding bugs.

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Mon Nov 17, 2008 1:50 am It was actually a thermonuclear explosion and a power loader that sealed the deal...

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Tue Nov 25, 2008 7:24 pm You sort ofhave to wonder at the mind that produced the flamethrower, though. What kind of thought pattern would possibly reach the conclusion of "Y'know, I really want to set that thing on fire, but it's all the way over there. I need something to allow me to set it on fire from over here " ?

[]:             Quote Post by Count A'ight » Fri Nov 28, 2008 10:43 pm The idea was probably concocted by some caveman who settled his disputes by throwing burning pieces of wood at other cavemen.
